meridian_skill.description:
Run `gbrain skillpack-check` to produce an agent-readable JSON health report
for the gbrain install. Wraps `gbrain doctor` + `gbrain apply-migrations
--list` so a host agent (your OpenClaw's morning-briefing, any OpenClaw cron)
can see at a glance whether the skillpack needs attention.

Use when the user asks "is gbrain healthy?", when a cron fires a morning
check, or proactively when something seems off (jobs not running, brain
not updating, autopilot silent).
meridian_skill.name: skillpack-check

outline.text: What to do with the output

sections.heading: Contract
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

checklist:ai-autonomy:YDBgIOKAlCBoZWFsdGh5LCBub3RoaW5nIHRvIGRvCmAxYCDigJQgYWN0aW9uIG5lZWRlZC4gUmVhZCBgYWN0aW9uc1tdYCBhbmQgZXhlY3V0ZQpgMmAg4oCUIGNvdWxkIG5vdCBkZXRlcm1pbmUgKGJpbmFyeSBjcmFzaCBvciBtaXNzaW5nIHN1YmNvbW1hbmQpLiBJbnZlc3RpZ2F0ZQ==

sections.heading: When to run
lines: use judgment to follow the When to run guidance:
item: **Daily cron** (e.g. your OpenClaw's `morning-briefing`): `gbrain skillpack-check --quiet`
Exit code alone tells you if anything is wrong; surface a one-liner in the
briefing only when exit != 0. No JSON noise in happy-path briefings
item: **On demand**: `gbrain skillpack-check` for the full JSON when debugging
item: **In a CI pipeline**: same pattern — exit code gates, JSON is the evidence

sections.heading: What to do with the output
Happy path (`healthy: true`)
lines: Surface the summary in the agent's output only if asked. Nothing else

sections.heading: Action needed (`healthy: false`)
lines: use judgment to follow the Action needed (`healthy: false`) guidance:
The `actions[]` array contains the commands to run, in order. Execute them:
codeblock:bash:Zm9yIGNtZCBpbiAkKGVjaG8gIiRSRVBPUlQiIHwganEgLXIgJy5hY3Rpb25zW10nKTsgZG8KICBldmFsICIkY21kIgpkb25l
Common `actions[]` entries and what they mean:
item: `gbrain apply-migrations --yes` — A migration is pending or half-finished
Run this (it's idempotent). If it exits `status: "partial"`, the host has
non-builtin cron handlers that need plugin registration — follow
`skills/migrations/v0.11.0.md`
item: `gbrain embed --stale` — Embeddings are stale
item: `gbrain check-backlinks --fix` — Dead links or missing back-links
item: Free-text action (no `Run:` prefix in the source message) — agent judgment
needed. Quote it in the report for the user

sections.heading: Determine failure (`exit 2`)
lines: Treat as urgent. Probably means the gbrain binary is missing from `$PATH` or
a required subcommand crashed. Check:
`which gbrain` returns a path
`gbrain --version` exits 0
`~/.gbrain/` is accessible

sections.heading: Output format
lines: codeblock:json:

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

sections.heading: Output Format
lines: The skill itself doesn't write files; it reports the CLI output verbatim to
the user (or to the agent's briefing pipeline). One-line summary first,
then the action list, then (only if relevant) the full JSON for debugging

sections.heading: Related
lines: `gbrain doctor` — the underlying filesystem + DB check. skillpack-check
composes this
`gbrain apply-migrations --list` — the migration status view
`skills/migrations/v0.11.0.md` — the host-agent instruction manual for
resolving `pending-host-work.jsonl` items
`docs/guides/minions-fix.md` — troubleshooting a half-migrated install

meridian_skill.triggers:
"skillpack check"
